tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read any file in the repository. Use before editing to ensure you have the current content. Always read a file before making changes to it.

tools.name: create_file
tools.description: Create a new file at the exact path defined in FOLDER_STRUCTURE.md. Never create files in paths not defined in FOLDER_STRUCTURE.md without flagging it first.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Edit an existing file using precise string replacement. Always read the file first. Always make the smallest change needed — do not rewrite entire files when a targeted edit will do.

tools.name: run_command
tools.description: Execute shell commands for: running tests, starting services, running Alembic migrations, running seeding scripts, verifying curl responses, checking Redis keys, checking database records. Use this to run every verification step in STEPS_BACKEND.md.

tools.name: search_codebase
tools.description: Search the codebase for a function name, class name, variable, or string. Use before creating a new function to check if it already exists. Use when tracing how a piece of data flows through the system.

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List files in a directory. Use to verify the directory structure matches FOLDER_STRUCTURE.md before and after creating files.

tool_use_rules: Always read a file before editing it — never edit from memory.
Always run verification commands after completing each STEPS_BACKEND.md step.
Never run destructive database commands (DROP TABLE, DELETE without WHERE) without explicit confirmation.
When running curl for verification, always include the -v flag to see response headers.
When a test fails, read the full error output before making any changes.
Never install packages not in requirements.txt without flagging it first.

restricted_tools.name: create_file
restricted_tools.restriction: Do not create any file under apps/web/ — that is the Frontend Agent's domain.

restricted_tools.name: edit_file
restricted_tools.restriction: Do not edit any file under apps/web/ — that is the Frontend Agent's domain.
